title: Does the cyclic group C_419 admit a (419,133,42)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: The exact tuple is already settled in prior art, and the local proof attempt is unsound at the multiplier-certification step. Lean would formalize a rediscovery rather than close a frontier packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Fails the micro-paper lane on verify: the exact tuple is already eliminated in prior art, and the local t=3 orbit argument misapplies Baumert-Gordon Theorem 3.2.
family_affinity: High affinity with the Baumert-Gordon cyclic-survivor multiplier-orbit lane.
generalization_signal: The local arithmetic setup may still be useful background, but the claimed 209-orbit proof does not transfer because the multiplier theorem was used in the wrong direction.
strongest_honest_claim: The cyclic group C_419 does not admit a (419,133,42) difference set, but this run did not discover that fact: bounded prior-art audit found it already settled in Leung-Ma-Schmidt, Example 5.4, and the local proof attempt fails at the multiplier-certification step.
candidate_theorem_slice: Prior art already gives the exact slice: Example 5.4 in Leung-Ma-Schmidt eliminates the cyclic (419,133,42) row via Corollary 4.1 with multiplier 13 and a quick computer search.
